observations.summary: The laugh is metaphorically described as the sound of mountains of bedrock grinding against each other deep within the earth, conveying a slow, foundational, and inevitable force.

evidence.quote: The laugh that followed was the sound of mountains of bedrock grinding against each other in the deeps of the earth—slow, foundational, utterly certain it would arrive.

observations.notes: This metaphor emphasizes the inevitability and deep-rooted nature of the laugh, suggesting a powerful underlying force or event.
